articles.headline: Parliament Passes Landmark National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026
articles.explainer:
• The Bill aims to streamline skill development initiatives, align training with industry demands, and boost employment opportunities across various sectors.
• Key provisions include the establishment of a National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB) and a dedicated fund for vocational training.
• It introduces a framework for certification of prior learning and promotes apprenticeships in both traditional and emerging industries.
• The legislation emphasizes public-private partnerships to bridge the skill gap and enhance employability, particularly for youth and women.
• Focuses on leveraging technology for skill delivery and assessment, ensuring inclusivity and accessibility.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026 passed by Parliament.
Establishes National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB).
Focuses on public-private partnerships and technology leverage.
Aims to certify prior learning and promote apprenticeships.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential impact of the Bill on India's demographic dividend and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges in effective implementation of skill development policies in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate how the Bill addresses the existing skill gap and unemployment issues.
Examine the role of technology and public-private partnerships as envisioned by the Bill.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building, symbolic of legislative action.
articles.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations globally, with over 65% of its population under 35, making skill development crucial for economic growth.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of New Digital Personal Data Protection Rules
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court dismissed a batch of petitions challenging the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025, affirming their constitutional validity.
• The ruling emphasizes the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• The court highlighted the robust grievance redressal mechanism and penalties for data breaches as crucial safeguards.
• This decision provides legal certainty for technology companies and data fiduciaries operating in India.
• The rules are expected to have significant implications for how personal data is collected, processed, and stored across various sectors.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.
Decision balances privacy rights and state interests.
Rules include grievance redressal and penalties for breaches.
Impacts data collection, processing, and storage in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's digital economy and individual privacy rights.
Discuss the challenges in enforcing data protection laws in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules in safeguarding citizens' data.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's rules compare.
articles.imageDescription: Scales of justice in front of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: India is home to the world's second-largest internet user base, making robust data protection laws critically important.

articles.headline: India and Japan Bolster Strategic Ties with Agreements on Critical Minerals and Green Energy
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan signed multiple Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) focusing on cooperation in critical minerals and green energy technologies.
• The agreements aim to diversify supply chains for rare earth elements and strategic minerals, reducing dependence on single sources.
• Both nations pledged to collaborate on developing renewable energy projects, hydrogen technology, and energy storage solutions.
• This partnership underscores a shared commitment to energy security, climate action, and resilient economic growth in the Indo-Pacific.
• The move is seen as a significant step towards strengthening economic and strategic alignment between the two countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan signed MoUs on critical minerals and green energy.
Aims to diversify supply chains for rare earth elements.
Collaboration on renewable energy, hydrogen, and energy storage.
Strengthens economic and strategic alignment in Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Japan cooperation in critical minerals for global supply chain resilience.
Discuss how this partnership contributes to India's energy transition goals and climate commitments.
Examine the broader implications of this collaboration for the Indo-Pacific region's stability and economic development.
Evaluate the potential for future technological transfer and joint research in green energy between India and Japan.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together.
articles.trivia: Japan is India's 5th largest trading partner, and both countries are part of Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad).

articles.headline: Alarming Rate of Himalayan Glacier Melt Highlighted in New Environmental Report
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences details an accelerating rate of glacier retreat across the Indian Himalayas.
• The study attributes the rapid melting primarily to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• Consequences include increased risk of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) and long-term freshwater scarcity for downstream communities.
• The report calls for urgent policy interventions, including enhanced monitoring, regional cooperation, and aggressive climate mitigation strategies.
• It emphasizes the need for sustainable water management practices and adaptation measures in vulnerable Himalayan regions.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Report details accelerating glacier retreat in Indian Himalayas.
Attributed to rising temperatures and black carbon.
Increases risk of GLOFs and freshwater scarcity.
Calls for urgent climate mitigation and adaptation measures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of Himalayan glacier melt on India's environment, economy, and society.
Analyze the challenges in implementing effective climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies in mountainous regions.
Evaluate the role of international and regional cooperation in addressing transboundary environmental issues like glacier melt.
Suggest policy recommendations for sustainable water resource management in the context of changing Himalayan hydrology.
articles.imageDescription: A receding glacier in the Himalayas with visible meltwater.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often called the 'Third Pole' due to their vast ice reserves, which feed major Asian rivers.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Propulsion System for Gaganyaan Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground test of a new high-thrust liquid propulsion engine.
• This advanced propulsion system is designed to enhance the capabilities and reliability of the Gaganyaan mission's orbital module.
• The test marks a crucial milestone in India's human spaceflight program, demonstrating indigenous technological prowess.
• The new engine promises improved thrust-to-weight ratio and specific impulse, crucial for longer duration missions.
• ISRO continues to push boundaries in space technology, aiming for India's first manned space mission by 2027.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a new high-thrust liquid propulsion engine.
Engine designed for Gaganyaan mission's orbital module.
Crucial milestone for India's human spaceflight program.
Aims for improved thrust-to-weight ratio and specific impulse.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous propulsion technology for India's space program and strategic autonomy.
Analyze the challenges and future prospects of India's human spaceflight mission (Gaganyaan).
Evaluate the broader socio-economic and technological benefits of advancements in space technology for India.
Examine how ISRO's achievements contribute to India's position as a global space power.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of a rocket engine firing during a test.
articles.trivia: Gaganyaan will make India the fourth nation to independently send humans to space, after Russia, USA, and China.

articles.headline: RBI Unveils New Regulatory Framework for Fintech Lending Platforms
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released comprehensive guidelines for regulating the rapidly growing fintech lending sector.
• The framework aims to enhance consumer protection, promote fair practices, and ensure financial stability in digital lending.
• Key measures include mandating transparent interest rates, disclosure of all charges, and a robust grievance redressal mechanism.
• It also introduces stricter norms for data privacy and security, addressing concerns over predatory lending practices.
• The move is expected to bring greater accountability and sustainability to India's burgeoning online lending market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: RBI released new guidelines for fintech lending platforms.
Aims for consumer protection and financial stability.
Mandates transparent interest rates and grievance redressal.
Introduces stricter norms for data privacy and security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for a comprehensive regulatory framework for fintech lending in India, considering its rapid growth and associated risks.
Analyze the potential impact of RBI's new guidelines on both fintech companies and consumers.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing innovation with regulation in the digital financial services sector.
Examine how these regulations contribute to financial inclusion and stability.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo over a backdrop of digital financial graphs.
articles.trivia: India's fintech market is projected to reach $1 trillion in transaction value by 2025.

articles.headline: BSF Intercepts Major Drone Infiltration Attempt Along Western Border, Recovers Narcotics and Weapons
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) successfully thwarted a significant drone-based infiltration bid from across the Western border.
• Security forces recovered a large consignment of narcotics, including heroin, and a cache of weapons and ammunition.
• This incident highlights the evolving threat of drone technology being used for cross-border smuggling and terror activities.
• BSF has intensified surveillance and deployed anti-drone technologies to counter such threats effectively.
• The operation underscores the continuous challenges faced by India's border security forces in maintaining territorial integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: BSF intercepted a drone infiltration attempt on the Western border.
Recovered narcotics, weapons, and ammunition.
Highlights evolving threat of drone-based smuggling.
BSF intensifying surveillance and deploying anti-drone tech.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of drone technology in cross-border smuggling and its implications for India's internal security.
Analyze the measures taken by Indian security forces to counter drone-based threats along international borders.
Evaluate the challenges faced by border guarding forces in managing porous borders and combating organized crime.
Suggest strategies for enhancing border security infrastructure and technological capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: BSF personnel inspecting a recovered drone and contraband.
articles.trivia: India shares a 3,323 km long border with Pakistan, which is prone to various security challenges.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' to Combat Child Malnutrition
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan', a national initiative to intensively tackle child malnutrition.
• The campaign focuses on a multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, sanitation, education, and food security programs.
• It aims to improve nutritional outcomes for children, adolescent girls, and pregnant and lactating mothers.
• Key strategies include community-based management of acute malnutrition, promotion of dietary diversity, and early childhood care.
• The Abhiyan seeks to leverage technology for real-time monitoring and public awareness campaigns to foster behavioral change.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Ministry of WCD launched 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan'.
Aims to combat child malnutrition through multi-sectoral approach.
Targets children, adolescent girls, pregnant/lactating mothers.
Emphasizes community management and technology for monitoring.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors contributing to child malnutrition in India and the challenges in addressing them.
Analyze the 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan's' multi-sectoral approach and its potential effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological intervention in achieving better nutritional outcomes.
Suggest additional measures to strengthen India's fight against malnutrition.
articles.imageDescription: Children participating in a healthy eating program.
articles.trivia: India accounts for a significant portion of the world's malnourished children, despite improvements in recent years.

articles.headline: Veteran Gujarat MLA Govind Parmar Passes Away at 72
articles.explainer:
• Govind Parmar, a prominent political figure and former Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Gujarat, passed away at the age of 72.
• He served as an MLA for two terms, from 1995-2003 and then again since 2017, representing the [Constituency Name] constituency.
• Parmar was known for his dedication to public service and his contributions to regional development in Gujarat.
• His demise marks the end of a significant political career spanning several decades.
• He was highly respected across party lines for his measured approach and deep understanding of local issues.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Govind Parmar, former Gujarat MLA, passed away at 72.
Served as MLA from 1995-2003 and since 2017.
Known for public service and regional development contributions.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Govind Parmar.
articles.trivia: Gujarat Legislative Assembly consists of 182 members elected for a five-year term.

articles.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates New Phase of Sagarmala Project, Boosting Coastal Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways inaugurated the third phase of the Sagarmala Programme.
• This phase focuses on developing new port-linked industrial clusters and enhancing multi-modal connectivity, including inland waterways.
• The project aims to reduce logistics costs, boost coastal shipping, and promote port-led industrialization.
• Significant investments are earmarked for upgrading existing ports and constructing new jetties and terminals.
• The initiative is expected to generate substantial employment and contribute to the 'Make in India' vision by facilitating trade.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
articles.source: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways
prelimsPoints: Union Minister inaugurated Sagarmala Programme Phase III.
Focuses on port-linked industrial clusters and multi-modal connectivity.
Aims to reduce logistics costs and boost coastal shipping.
Expected to generate employment and facilitate trade.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Sagarmala Programme in enhancing India's maritime trade and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with developing port infrastructure and coastal connectivity.
Evaluate the potential of inland waterways to transform India's logistics landscape.
Examine how the Sagarmala project aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A modern port with cargo ships and cranes.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, with 13 major ports and 200 non-major ports.

articles.headline: Government Introduces New Subsidy Scheme to Promote Organic Farming Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare unveiled a new subsidy scheme aimed at encouraging widespread adoption of organic farming.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for organic certification, input procurement, and marketing of organic produce.
• It seeks to enhance soil health, reduce chemical fertilizer use, and improve farmers' income through premium pricing.
• Training and capacity building programs for farmers on sustainable organic practices are also a core component.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to sustainable agriculture and ecological balance.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Subsidies directly and indirectly related to agriculture; minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Agriculture launched new organic farming subsidy scheme.
Provides financial aid for certification, inputs, and marketing.
Aims to enhance soil health, reduce chemical use, and improve farmer income.
Includes training and capacity building for farmers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities for promoting organic farming in India's agricultural sector.
Analyze the potential impact of government subsidies on the adoption rate of organic practices and farmer incomes.
Evaluate how organic farming contributes to environmental sustainability and food security.
Examine the role of policy support, market linkages, and consumer awareness in scaling up organic agriculture.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer tending to an organic field.
articles.trivia: Sikkim was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Buddhist Monastery Ruins in Ladakh
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery in a remote part of Ladakh.
• Preliminary excavations suggest the site dates back to the 7th-8th century CE, indicating early Buddhist presence in the region.
• The findings include intricate murals, stupas, and monastic cells, offering new insights into the spread of Buddhism in the Himalayas.
• This discovery holds immense historical and cultural significance, potentially revising understandings of Ladakh's ancient past.
• Further research and conservation efforts are planned to protect and study the newly found heritage site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient Buddhist monastery ruins in Ladakh.
Site dates back to 7th-8th century CE.
Findings include murals, stupas, and monastic cells.
Offers insights into early Buddhism in the Himalayas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new archaeological discoveries in understanding India's cultural and religious history.
Analyze the role of Ladakh as a historical crossroads for the spread of Buddhism and its cultural exchanges.
Evaluate the challenges involved in excavating and preserving ancient sites in remote and harsh geographical conditions.
Examine how such discoveries contribute to local tourism and heritage awareness.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists at work at an excavation site in a mountainous region.
articles.trivia: Ladakh, often called 'Little Tibet', is known for its rich Buddhist culture and numerous ancient monasteries.

articles.headline: New Public Health Campaign Launched to Raise Awareness About Non-Communicable Diseases
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a nationwide public health campaign focused on preventing and managing Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign targets lifestyle changes, promoting healthy diets, regular physical activity, and discouraging tobacco and alcohol consumption.
• It aims to educate the public on early detection, risk factors, and the importance of regular health check-ups for NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular diseases.
• Special emphasis is placed on reaching rural populations and integrating NCD screening into primary healthcare services.
• This initiative seeks to reduce the burden of NCDs, which are a leading cause of mortality and morbidity in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Health launched NCD awareness campaign.
Promotes healthy diets, physical activity, discourages tobacco/alcohol.
Focuses on early detection and regular check-ups.
Targets rural populations and integrates NCD screening into primary healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the effectiveness of public health campaigns in promoting behavioral changes for NCD prevention.
Evaluate the challenges in integrating NCD prevention and management into India's existing healthcare infrastructure.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for reducing the prevalence of NCDs at both individual and community levels.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting healthy lifestyle choices.
articles.trivia: NCDs account for over 60% of all deaths in India, with cardiovascular diseases and diabetes being major contributors.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Committee Submits Interim Report
articles.explainer:
• The high-level committee appointed to review the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 submitted its interim report.
• The report highlights significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integration of technology in education.
• It also identifies key challenges, particularly in infrastructure development and ensuring equitable access in remote areas.
• Recommendations include accelerated faculty recruitment, increased public spending on education, and greater autonomy for higher education institutions.
• The interim findings will guide the Ministry of Education in refining strategies for the full realization of NEP's goals.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 Implementation Review Committee submitted interim report.
Highlights progress in curriculum, teacher training, technology integration.
Identifies challenges in infrastructure and equitable access.
Recommends accelerated faculty recruitment and increased public spending.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Analyze the key achievements and challenges in the initial phases of NEP 2020 implementation.
Evaluate the recommendations of the review committee in addressing the gaps and accelerating policy realization.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to foster a holistic and multidisciplinary approach to education.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after those of 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Phase III Launched with Focus on Sustainable Urban Mobility
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the third phase of the Smart Cities Mission, with a renewed emphasis on sustainable urban mobility.
• This phase will prioritize projects related to integrated public transport systems, electric vehicle infrastructure, and pedestrian-friendly zones.
• The mission aims to improve the quality of urban life, reduce carbon emissions, and promote green infrastructure development.
• New funding mechanisms and technology adoption for intelligent traffic management and smart parking solutions are included.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to building livable, resilient, and environmentally friendly urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission Phase III launched, focusing on sustainable urban mobility.
Prioritizes public transport, EV infrastructure, and pedestrian zones.
Aims to improve urban life, reduce carbon emissions, and promote green infrastructure.
Includes funding for intelligent traffic management and smart parking.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and objectives of India's Smart Cities Mission, specifically highlighting the shift towards sustainable urban mobility.
Analyze the challenges in implementing smart city projects, particularly in integrating various urban systems.
Evaluate the potential impact of electric vehicle infrastructure and public transport improvements on urban pollution and livability.
Examine the role of technology and citizen participation in achieving the goals of the Smart Cities Mission.
articles.imageDescription: A modern city skyline with green areas and efficient transport.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 with the objective of promoting cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life.

highlights: Parliament passes the landmark National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026, aiming to bridge skill gaps and boost youth employment.
Supreme Court upholds the constitutional validity of the new Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025, providing clarity for data governance.
India and Japan sign strategic agreements on critical minerals and green energy, strengthening cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
New environmental report reveals an alarming acceleration of Himalayan glacier melt, prompting calls for urgent climate action and adaptation.
ISRO successfully tests a next-generation propulsion system for the Gaganyaan mission, marking a key milestone in India's human spaceflight program.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recently passed National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026, consider the following statements:
1. It mandates the establishment of a National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB).
2. It focuses exclusively on formal sector skill development, excluding traditional apprenticeships.
3. The Bill emphasizes public-private partnerships for skill delivery and assessment.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Bill aims to establish a National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB). Statement 2 is incorrect: The Bill promotes apprenticeships in both traditional and emerging industries, not excluding traditional ones. Statement 3 is correct: It emphasizes public-private partnerships to bridge the skill gap.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies recently upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that the Supreme Court dismissed petitions and affirmed the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.

prelimsMocks.question:
India and Japan recently signed agreements on cooperation in which of the following areas?
1. Critical Minerals
2. Green Energy Technologies
3. Space Exploration
4. Defence Manufacturing
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article explicitly mentions agreements focusing on cooperation in critical minerals and green energy technologies. While space and defence are areas of cooperation, the specific agreements highlighted in the news for this date were critical minerals and green energy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' recently launched by the Government of India:
1. It is an initiative of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
2. It employs a multi-sectoral approach integrating health, sanitation, and food security.
3. It focuses exclusively on children under the age of five.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' was launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development, not the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare. Statement 2 is correct: The campaign focuses on a multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, sanitation, education, and food security programs. Statement 3 is incorrect: It aims to improve nutritional outcomes for children, adolescent girls, and pregnant and lactating mothers, not exclusively children under five.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Sagarmala Programme Phase III is/are correct?
1. It primarily focuses on promoting tourism along India's coastline.
2. It aims to develop new port-linked industrial clusters.
3. It includes enhancing multi-modal connectivity, including inland waterways.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: While tourism might be a tangential benefit, the primary focus of Sagarmala Phase III is on port-linked industrial clusters and logistics, not primarily tourism. Statements 2 and 3 are correct: The article explicitly states that this phase focuses on developing new port-linked industrial clusters and enhancing multi-modal connectivity, including inland waterways.

mainsMocks.question: The National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026, seeks to address India's demographic dividend and unemployment challenges. Discuss the key provisions of the Bill and critically evaluate its potential impact on the nation's human capital development and economic growth. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Bill's objectives. Detail key provisions like NSEB, apprenticeships, PPP model, technology leverage. Analyze potential benefits (skill gap reduction, employability, economic growth) and potential challenges (implementation, funding, infrastructure, inclusivity). Conclude with a balanced perspective on its significance.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025, marks a significant step in India's data governance framework. Discuss the balance struck by these rules between individual privacy and legitimate state/corporate interests. What are the major implications of this ruling for citizens and the digital economy? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Explain the context of the ruling and the essence of data protection. Detail how the rules balance privacy (e.g., consent, data principal rights) with interests (e.g., legitimate uses, data fiduciaries). Discuss implications for citizens (privacy, grievance redressal) and the digital economy (compliance, innovation, trust). Conclude on the evolving landscape of digital rights.

mainsMocks.question: The accelerating rate of Himalayan glacier melt poses severe environmental and socio-economic risks for India. Analyze the primary drivers of this phenomenon and its multi-faceted consequences. Suggest comprehensive strategies for both mitigation and adaptation to safeguard the Himalayan ecosystem and dependent communities. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by stating the gravity of glacier melt. Identify drivers: global warming, black carbon, local factors. Discuss consequences: GLOFs, water scarcity, ecological imbalance, impact on livelihoods. Propose mitigation strategies (global climate action, emission reduction) and adaptation measures (early warning systems, water resource management, resilient infrastructure, regional cooperation). Conclude on the urgency of integrated action.

quickBytes.text: The National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026, aims to establish a National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB).
Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.
India and Japan signed MoUs on critical minerals and green energy, enhancing Indo-Pacific cooperation.
A new report highlighted the alarming rate of glacier melt in the Indian Himalayas, linked to climate change.
ISRO successfully tested a next-generation propulsion system for the Gaganyaan mission.
RBI unveiled new regulatory framework for fintech lending platforms to enhance consumer protection.
The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' to combat child malnutrition.
Govind Parmar, a veteran MLA from Gujarat, passed away at the age of 72.
ASI discovered ancient Buddhist monastery ruins in a remote part of Ladakh, dating back to 7th-8th century CE.
The Smart Cities Mission Phase III was launched, focusing on sustainable urban mobility and green infrastructure.

• Preliminary excavations suggest the site dates back to the 7th-8th century CE, indicating early Buddhist presence in the region.
• The findings include intricate murals, stupas, and monastic cells, offering new insights into the spread of Buddhism in the Himalayas.
• This discovery holds immense historical and cultural significance, potentially revising understandings of Ladakh's ancient past.
• Further research and conservation efforts are planned to protect and study the newly found heritage site.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

Site dates back to 7th-8th century CE.
Findings include murals, stupas, and monastic cells.

Analyze the role of Ladakh as a historical crossroads for the spread of Buddhism and its cultural exchanges.

GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists at work at an excavation site in a mountainous region.
GS1.trivia: Ladakh, often called 'Little Tibet', is known for its rich Buddhist culture and numerous ancient monasteries.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Landmark National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026
GS2.explainer:
• The Bill aims to streamline skill development initiatives, align training with industry demands, and boost employment opportunities across various sectors.
• Key provisions include the establishment of a National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB) and a dedicated fund for vocational training.
• It introduces a framework for certification of prior learning and promotes apprenticeships in both traditional and emerging industries.
• The legislation emphasizes public-private partnerships to bridge the skill gap and enhance employability, particularly for youth and women.
• Focuses on leveraging technology for skill delivery and assessment, ensuring inclusivity and accessibility.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: National Skill Development and Employment Generation Bill, 2026 passed by Parliament.
Establishes National Skill and Employment Board (NSEB).
Focuses on public-private partnerships and technology leverage.
Aims to certify prior learning and promote apprenticeships.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential impact of the Bill on India's demographic dividend and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges in effective implementation of skill development policies in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate how the Bill addresses the existing skill gap and unemployment issues.
Examine the role of technology and public-private partnerships as envisioned by the Bill.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building, symbolic of legislative action.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the youngest populations globally, with over 65% of its population under 35, making skill development crucial for economic growth.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of New Digital Personal Data Protection Rules
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court dismissed a batch of petitions challenging the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025, affirming their constitutional validity.
• The ruling emphasizes the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests in data processing.
• The court highlighted the robust grievance redressal mechanism and penalties for data breaches as crucial safeguards.
• This decision provides legal certainty for technology companies and data fiduciaries operating in India.
• The rules are expected to have significant implications for how personal data is collected, processed, and stored across various sectors.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025.
Decision balances privacy rights and state interests.
Rules include grievance redressal and penalties for breaches.
Impacts data collection, processing, and storage in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's digital economy and individual privacy rights.
Discuss the challenges in enforcing data protection laws in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules in safeguarding citizens' data.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's rules compare.
GS2.imageDescription: Scales of justice in front of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: India is home to the world's second-largest internet user base, making robust data protection laws critically important.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Bolster Strategic Ties with Agreements on Critical Minerals and Green Energy
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan signed multiple Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) focusing on cooperation in critical minerals and green energy technologies.
• The agreements aim to diversify supply chains for rare earth elements and strategic minerals, reducing dependence on single sources.
• Both nations pledged to collaborate on developing renewable energy projects, hydrogen technology, and energy storage solutions.
• This partnership underscores a shared commitment to energy security, climate action, and resilient economic growth in the Indo-Pacific.
• The move is seen as a significant step towards strengthening economic and strategic alignment between the two countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and Japan signed MoUs on critical minerals and green energy.
Aims to diversify supply chains for rare earth elements.
Collaboration on renewable energy, hydrogen, and energy storage.
Strengthens economic and strategic alignment in Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Japan cooperation in critical minerals for global supply chain resilience.
Discuss how this partnership contributes to India's energy transition goals and climate commitments.
Examine the broader implications of this collaboration for the Indo-Pacific region's stability and economic development.
Evaluate the potential for future technological transfer and joint research in green energy between India and Japan.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed together.
GS2.trivia: Japan is India's 5th largest trading partner, and both countries are part of Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad).

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan' to Combat Child Malnutrition
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan', a national initiative to intensively tackle child malnutrition.
• The campaign focuses on a multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, sanitation, education, and food security programs.
• It aims to improve nutritional outcomes for children, adolescent girls, and pregnant and lactating mothers.
• Key strategies include community-based management of acute malnutrition, promotion of dietary diversity, and early childhood care.
• The Abhiyan seeks to leverage technology for real-time monitoring and public awareness campaigns to foster behavioral change.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Ministry of WCD launched 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan'.
Aims to combat child malnutrition through multi-sectoral approach.
Targets children, adolescent girls, pregnant/lactating mothers.
Emphasizes community management and technology for monitoring.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors contributing to child malnutrition in India and the challenges in addressing them.
Analyze the 'Poshan Shakti Abhiyan's' multi-sectoral approach and its potential effectiveness.
Evaluate the role of community participation and technological intervention in achieving better nutritional outcomes.
Suggest additional measures to strengthen India's fight against malnutrition.
GS2.imageDescription: Children participating in a healthy eating program.
GS2.trivia: India accounts for a significant portion of the world's malnourished children, despite improvements in recent years.

GS2.headline: Veteran Gujarat MLA Govind Parmar Passes Away at 72
GS2.explainer:
• Govind Parmar, a prominent political figure and former Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Gujarat, passed away at the age of 72.
• He served as an MLA for two terms, from 1995-2003 and then again since 2017, representing the [Constituency Name] constituency.
• Parmar was known for his dedication to public service and his contributions to regional development in Gujarat.
• His demise marks the end of a significant political career spanning several decades.
• He was highly respected across party lines for his measured approach and deep understanding of local issues.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Govind Parmar, former Gujarat MLA, passed away at 72.
Served as MLA from 1995-2003 and since 2017.
Known for public service and regional development contributions.
GS2.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Govind Parmar.
GS2.trivia: Gujarat Legislative Assembly consists of 182 members elected for a five-year term.

GS2.headline: New Public Health Campaign Launched to Raise Awareness About Non-Communicable Diseases
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a nationwide public health campaign focused on preventing and managing Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign targets lifestyle changes, promoting healthy diets, regular physical activity, and discouraging tobacco and alcohol consumption.
• It aims to educate the public on early detection, risk factors, and the importance of regular health check-ups for NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular diseases.
• Special emphasis is placed on reaching rural populations and integrating NCD screening into primary healthcare services.
• This initiative seeks to reduce the burden of NCDs, which are a leading cause of mortality and morbidity in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Health launched NCD awareness campaign.
Promotes healthy diets, physical activity, discourages tobacco/alcohol.
Focuses on early detection and regular check-ups.
Targets rural populations and integrates NCD screening into primary healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the effectiveness of public health campaigns in promoting behavioral changes for NCD prevention.
Evaluate the challenges in integrating NCD prevention and management into India's existing healthcare infrastructure.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for reducing the prevalence of NCDs at both individual and community levels.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting healthy lifestyle choices.
GS2.trivia: NCDs account for over 60% of all deaths in India, with cardiovascular diseases and diabetes being major contributors.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Committee Submits Interim Report
GS2.explainer:
• The high-level committee appointed to review the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 submitted its interim report.
• The report highlights significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integration of technology in education.
• It also identifies key challenges, particularly in infrastructure development and ensuring equitable access in remote areas.
• Recommendations include accelerated faculty recruitment, increased public spending on education, and greater autonomy for higher education institutions.
• The interim findings will guide the Ministry of Education in refining strategies for the full realization of NEP's goals.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 Implementation Review Committee submitted interim report.
Highlights progress in curriculum, teacher training, technology integration.
Identifies challenges in infrastructure and equitable access.
Recommends accelerated faculty recruitment and increased public spending.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Analyze the key achievements and challenges in the initial phases of NEP 2020 implementation.
Evaluate the recommendations of the review committee in addressing the gaps and accelerating policy realization.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to foster a holistic and multidisciplinary approach to education.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, after those of 1968 and 1986.

GS3.headline: Alarming Rate of Himalayan Glacier Melt Highlighted in New Environmental Report
GS3.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences details an accelerating rate of glacier retreat across the Indian Himalayas.
• The study attributes the rapid melting primarily to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• Consequences include increased risk of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) and long-term freshwater scarcity for downstream communities.
• The report calls for urgent policy interventions, including enhanced monitoring, regional cooperation, and aggressive climate mitigation strategies.
• It emphasizes the need for sustainable water management practices and adaptation measures in vulnerable Himalayan regions.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Report details accelerating glacier retreat in Indian Himalayas.
Attributed to rising temperatures and black carbon.
Increases risk of GLOFs and freshwater scarcity.
Calls for urgent climate mitigation and adaptation measures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of Himalayan glacier melt on India's environment, economy, and society.
Analyze the challenges in implementing effective climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies in mountainous regions.
Evaluate the role of international and regional cooperation in addressing transboundary environmental issues like glacier melt.
Suggest policy recommendations for sustainable water resource management in the context of changing Himalayan hydrology.
GS3.imageDescription: A receding glacier in the Himalayas with visible meltwater.
GS3.trivia: The Himalayas are often called the 'Third Pole' due to their vast ice reserves, which feed major Asian rivers.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Propulsion System for Gaganyaan Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground test of a new high-thrust liquid propulsion engine.
• This advanced propulsion system is designed to enhance the capabilities and reliability of the Gaganyaan mission's orbital module.
• The test marks a crucial milestone in India's human spaceflight program, demonstrating indigenous technological prowess.
• The new engine promises improved thrust-to-weight ratio and specific impulse, crucial for longer duration missions.
• ISRO continues to push boundaries in space technology, aiming for India's first manned space mission by 2027.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested a new high-thrust liquid propulsion engine.
Engine designed for Gaganyaan mission's orbital module.
Crucial milestone for India's human spaceflight program.
Aims for improved thrust-to-weight ratio and specific impulse.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous propulsion technology for India's space program and strategic autonomy.
Analyze the challenges and future prospects of India's human spaceflight mission (Gaganyaan).
Evaluate the broader socio-economic and technological benefits of advancements in space technology for India.
Examine how ISRO's achievements contribute to India's position as a global space power.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration of a rocket engine firing during a test.
GS3.trivia: Gaganyaan will make India the fourth nation to independently send humans to space, after Russia, USA, and China.

GS3.headline: RBI Unveils New Regulatory Framework for Fintech Lending Platforms
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released comprehensive guidelines for regulating the rapidly growing fintech lending sector.
• The framework aims to enhance consumer protection, promote fair practices, and ensure financial stability in digital lending.
• Key measures include mandating transparent interest rates, disclosure of all charges, and a robust grievance redressal mechanism.
• It also introduces stricter norms for data privacy and security, addressing concerns over predatory lending practices.
• The move is expected to bring greater accountability and sustainability to India's burgeoning online lending market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: RBI released new guidelines for fintech lending platforms.
Aims for consumer protection and financial stability.
Mandates transparent interest rates and grievance redressal.
Introduces stricter norms for data privacy and security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need for a comprehensive regulatory framework for fintech lending in India, considering its rapid growth and associated risks.
Analyze the potential impact of RBI's new guidelines on both fintech companies and consumers.
Evaluate the challenges in balancing innovation with regulation in the digital financial services sector.
Examine how these regulations contribute to financial inclusion and stability.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo over a backdrop of digital financial graphs.
GS3.trivia: India's fintech market is projected to reach $1 trillion in transaction value by 2025.

GS3.headline: BSF Intercepts Major Drone Infiltration Attempt Along Western Border, Recovers Narcotics and Weapons
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) successfully thwarted a significant drone-based infiltration bid from across the Western border.
• Security forces recovered a large consignment of narcotics, including heroin, and a cache of weapons and ammunition.
• This incident highlights the evolving threat of drone technology being used for cross-border smuggling and terror activities.
• BSF has intensified surveillance and deployed anti-drone technologies to counter such threats effectively.
• The operation underscores the continuous challenges faced by India's border security forces in maintaining territorial integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: BSF intercepted a drone infiltration attempt on the Western border.
Recovered narcotics, weapons, and ammunition.
Highlights evolving threat of drone-based smuggling.
BSF intensifying surveillance and deploying anti-drone tech.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of drone technology in cross-border smuggling and its implications for India's internal security.
Analyze the measures taken by Indian security forces to counter drone-based threats along international borders.
Evaluate the challenges faced by border guarding forces in managing porous borders and combating organized crime.
Suggest strategies for enhancing border security infrastructure and technological capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: BSF personnel inspecting a recovered drone and contraband.
GS3.trivia: India shares a 3,323 km long border with Pakistan, which is prone to various security challenges.

GS3.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates New Phase of Sagarmala Project, Boosting Coastal Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways inaugurated the third phase of the Sagarmala Programme.
• This phase focuses on developing new port-linked industrial clusters and enhancing multi-modal connectivity, including inland waterways.
• The project aims to reduce logistics costs, boost coastal shipping, and promote port-led industrialization.
• Significant investments are earmarked for upgrading existing ports and constructing new jetties and terminals.
• The initiative is expected to generate substantial employment and contribute to the 'Make in India' vision by facilitating trade.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
GS3.source: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways
prelimsPoints: Union Minister inaugurated Sagarmala Programme Phase III.
Focuses on port-linked industrial clusters and multi-modal connectivity.
Aims to reduce logistics costs and boost coastal shipping.
Expected to generate employment and facilitate trade.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Sagarmala Programme in enhancing India's maritime trade and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with developing port infrastructure and coastal connectivity.
Evaluate the potential of inland waterways to transform India's logistics landscape.
Examine how the Sagarmala project aligns with the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern port with cargo ships and cranes.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, with 13 major ports and 200 non-major ports.

GS3.headline: Government Introduces New Subsidy Scheme to Promote Organic Farming Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare unveiled a new subsidy scheme aimed at encouraging widespread adoption of organic farming.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for organic certification, input procurement, and marketing of organic produce.
• It seeks to enhance soil health, reduce chemical fertilizer use, and improve farmers' income through premium pricing.
• Training and capacity building programs for farmers on sustainable organic practices are also a core component.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to sustainable agriculture and ecological balance.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Subsidies directly and indirectly related to agriculture; minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
GS3.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Agriculture launched new organic farming subsidy scheme.
Provides financial aid for certification, inputs, and marketing.
Aims to enhance soil health, reduce chemical use, and improve farmer income.
Includes training and capacity building for farmers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities for promoting organic farming in India's agricultural sector.
Analyze the potential impact of government subsidies on the adoption rate of organic practices and farmer incomes.
Evaluate how organic farming contributes to environmental sustainability and food security.
Examine the role of policy support, market linkages, and consumer awareness in scaling up organic agriculture.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer tending to an organic field.
GS3.trivia: Sikkim was declared India's first fully organic state in 2016.

GS3.headline: Smart Cities Mission Phase III Launched with Focus on Sustainable Urban Mobility
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the third phase of the Smart Cities Mission, with a renewed emphasis on sustainable urban mobility.
• This phase will prioritize projects related to integrated public transport systems, electric vehicle infrastructure, and pedestrian-friendly zones.
• The mission aims to improve the quality of urban life, reduce carbon emissions, and promote green infrastructure development.
• New funding mechanisms and technology adoption for intelligent traffic management and smart parking solutions are included.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to building livable, resilient, and environmentally friendly urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission Phase III launched, focusing on sustainable urban mobility.
Prioritizes public transport, EV infrastructure, and pedestrian zones.
Aims to improve urban life, reduce carbon emissions, and promote green infrastructure.
Includes funding for intelligent traffic management and smart parking.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and objectives of India's Smart Cities Mission, specifically highlighting the shift towards sustainable urban mobility.
Analyze the challenges in implementing smart city projects, particularly in integrating various urban systems.
Evaluate the potential impact of electric vehicle infrastructure and public transport improvements on urban pollution and livability.
Examine the role of technology and citizen participation in achieving the goals of the Smart Cities Mission.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern city skyline with green areas and efficient transport.
GS3.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 with the objective of promoting cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life.
